description: To assist another in what they are doing or trying to do—adding your effort to theirs, making their task easier, enabling what they couldn't do alone. 'Kofe' is the fundamental verb of mutual aid, the recognition that together we can do more than separately.
sound_symbolism: The word is brief and ready, like help offered quickly. 'Ko' comes with velar directness, moving toward the one who needs aid. Then 'phe' /ɸe/ gives with breath, effort offered, assistance rendered.
grammatical_notes: As verb: 'mi ru kofe' (I help you). As noun: 'wela kofe' (good help). Can specify the task: 'mi ru lo riola kofe' (I help you with work). Broader than panoru (support); kofe implies active assistance in specific tasks.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Offering help builds community and trust. Asking for help acknowledges interdependence. Both are honored.
pillars.solarpunk-values: Mutual aid—helping each other without expectation of return—is foundational to resilient communities.
